CheckTitle: Function app redirects HTTP to HTTPS
Description: **Azure Function apps** redirect `HTTP` traffic to `HTTPS` when the `HTTPS Only` setting is enabled. This evaluation identifies Function apps that do not force secure transport by checking whether plaintext requests are automatically redirected to encrypted endpoints.
Risk: Function apps can expose **HTTP endpoints** that accept credentials, access keys, tokens, and request payloads. Leaving **HTTP accessible** enables **man-in-the-middle** interception, credential and function key theft, and response tampering, undermining **confidentiality** and **integrity** of data in transit.

Code.Other:
1. Sign in to the Azure portal and go to Function App
2. Select your function app
3. Go to Settings > Configuration > General settings and set HTTPS Only to On
4. Click Save
Code.Terraform:
```hcl
# Enforce HTTPS-only on a Function App
resource "azurerm_linux_function_app" "<example_resource_name>" {
  name                       = "<example_resource_name>"
  resource_group_name        = "<example_resource_name>"
  location                   = "<example_location>"
  service_plan_id            = "<example_resource_id>"
  storage_account_name       = "<example_resource_name>"
  storage_account_access_key = "<example_secret_value>"

  https_only = true # Critical: redirects HTTP to HTTPS
}
```

Recommendation.Text:
Enforce **HTTPS-only** for all Function apps.
- Use trusted certificates and require `TLS 1.2` or later
- Call functions only over `https://` endpoints and update any legacy `http` clients
- Minimize HTTP exposure via WAF/CDN or private access
Apply **defense in depth** to protect function keys and data in transit.

Notes: When it is enabled, every incoming HTTP request is redirected to the HTTPS port, adding an extra level of security for all requests made to the function app.
